## Post 1 — X/Twitter @ 08:30 ICT

**ID:** `ai-is-not-a-chatbot-problem-6a53d5`  
**Account:** @jeditrinupab (ID: 16969)  
**Source angle:** LF1 — AI as Founder Operating System (OpenAI DeployCo)

```
AI is not a chatbot problem.

It is an operating system problem.

Old way:
Give everyone ChatGPT and hope productivity goes up.

New way:
Pick one workflow. Define the input. Define the output. Define the approval point.

That is where AI starts generating real business results.
```

---

## Post 2 — LinkedIn @ 10:00 ICT

**ID:** `founder-ai-operating-system-24b4b6`  
**Account:** Trinupab Jiratritarn (ID: 19173)  
**Source angle:** LF1 — Founder AI OS + Thai SME workflows

```
Most founders are asking the wrong question about AI.

The question is not:
"How do I get my team to use more AI?"

The better question is:
"Which parts of the business should no longer depend on memory, mood, or manual follow-up?"

That is where the real work starts.

For Thai SMEs, AI becomes valuable when it is installed into repeatable workflows:

• Lead response
• Proposal writing
• Meeting summaries
• Sales follow-up
• Customer support
• Weekly reporting
• Internal SOP search

Old way:
A capable person remembers what to do.

New way:
The system makes the next step obvious.

This is the shift I think every founder needs to understand.

AI is not the advantage by itself.

The advantage is the operating system you build around it.
```

---

## Post 3 — X/Twitter @ 12:30 ICT

**ID:** `the-customer-has-changed-first-36d784`  
**Account:** @jeditrinupab (ID: 16969)  
**Source angle:** LF2 — 35+ customers use ChatGPT before buying

```
Your customer may adopt AI before your team does.

That changes the sale.

They compare faster.
They ask sharper questions.
They arrive with stronger objections.

If your website, admin team, and sales process still answer in the old rhythm, you look slow.

Make your business AI-readable.
```

---

## Post 4 — X/Twitter @ 20:30 ICT

**ID:** `brief-before-media-spend-f423ad`  
**Account:** @jeditrinupab (ID: 16969)  
**Source angle:** LF3 — Google Small Brief / AI ad brief before spending

```
Most SME ads fail before the ad account opens.

Not because the platform is bad.
Because the brief is weak.

Before spending 1 baht on media, ask AI for:

1. 5 customer pains
2. 5 offer angles
3. 10 hooks
4. 5 objections
5. 1 clear next step

Brief first. Spend second.
```
